---
title: "aria_nbv Implementation Overview"
format: html
---

> **Note**: High-level package navigation now lives in `aria_nbv_overview.qmd`. This page is the current owner map for the oracle labeling stack that produces RRI supervision.

::: {.callout-note title="Source anchors"}
- Orchestrator: [`OracleRriLabelerConfig`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/pipelines/oracle_rri_labeler.py#L46), [`OracleRriLabeler`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/pipelines/oracle_rri_labeler.py#L80)
- Candidate generation: [`CandidateViewGeneratorConfig`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/pose_generation/candidate_generation.py#L50), [`CandidateViewGenerator`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/pose_generation/candidate_generation.py#L319), [`candidate_generation_rules.py`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/pose_generation/candidate_generation_rules.py#L1)
- Rendering and backprojection: [`CandidateDepthRenderer`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/rendering/candidate_depth_renderer.py#L108), [`Pytorch3DDepthRenderer`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/rendering/pytorch3d_depth_renderer.py#L79), [`build_candidate_pointclouds`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/rendering/candidate_pointclouds.py#L64)
- Scoring and post-processing: [`OracleRRIConfig`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/rri_metrics/oracle_rri.py#L26), [`OracleRRI`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/rri_metrics/oracle_rri.py#L34), [`RriOrdinalBinner`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/rri_metrics/rri_binning.py#L88)
- Mesh preprocessing: [`MeshProcessSpec`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/data_handling/mesh_cache.py#L27), [`load_or_process_mesh`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/data_handling/mesh_cache.py#L132)
:::

# Current internal pipeline

The repo does not implement oracle RRI as a single monolith. The live pipeline
is a thin orchestrator around four owners:

1. [`OracleRriLabeler`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/pipelines/oracle_rri_labeler.py#L80) pulls a typed [`EfmSnippetView`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/data_handling/efm_views.py#L479), resolves mesh preprocessing, and wires the rest of the stages together.
2. [`CandidateViewGenerator`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/pose_generation/candidate_generation.py#L319) samples shell candidates and delegates geometric rejection to the rule objects in [`candidate_generation_rules.py`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/pose_generation/candidate_generation_rules.py#L1).
3. [`CandidateDepthRenderer`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/rendering/candidate_depth_renderer.py#L108) selects valid candidates and uses [`Pytorch3DDepthRenderer`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/rendering/pytorch3d_depth_renderer.py#L79) to render candidate depth maps.
4. [`build_candidate_pointclouds`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/rendering/candidate_pointclouds.py#L64) backprojects those depths, and [`OracleRRI`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/rri_metrics/oracle_rri.py#L34) turns the before/after point sets into per-candidate RRI diagnostics.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
    A[EfmSnippetView] --> B[OracleRriLabeler]
    B --> C[CandidateViewGenerator]
    C --> D[CandidateDepthRenderer]
    D --> E[build_candidate_pointclouds]
    E --> F[OracleRRI]
    F --> G[RriOrdinalBinner]
```

# Owner map

## Dataset and mesh preparation

- Typed snippet payloads come from [`AseEfmDataset`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/data_handling/efm_dataset.py#L320) and [`EfmSnippetView`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/data_handling/efm_views.py#L479).
- GT mesh cropping, simplification, and caching are owned by [`MeshProcessSpec`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/data_handling/mesh_cache.py#L27) and [`load_or_process_mesh`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/data_handling/mesh_cache.py#L132), not by the renderer itself.
- This separation matters: the same processed mesh is reused for collision checks, rendering, and scoring, so doc links should point to the mesh cache surface when discussing snippet-local GT preprocessing.

## Candidate sampling

- [`CandidateViewGeneratorConfig`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/pose_generation/candidate_generation.py#L50) owns the shell geometry and runtime toggles.
- [`PositionSampler`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/pose_generation/positional_sampling.py#L20) handles center sampling, while [`OrientationBuilder`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/pose_generation/orientations.py#L21) converts sampled centers into candidate camera poses.
- The rejection rules are intentionally factored into [`MinDistanceToMeshRule`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/pose_generation/candidate_generation_rules.py#L39), [`PathCollisionRule`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/pose_generation/candidate_generation_rules.py#L93), and [`FreeSpaceRule`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/pose_generation/candidate_generation_rules.py#L171).

## Depth rendering and candidate point clouds

- [`CandidateDepthRenderer`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/rendering/candidate_depth_renderer.py#L108) is the contract that the app and labeler both call.
- [`Pytorch3DDepthRenderer`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/rendering/pytorch3d_depth_renderer.py#L79) owns the actual rasterization path and camera slicing logic.
- [`build_candidate_pointclouds`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/rendering/candidate_pointclouds.py#L64) performs the NDC-aware backprojection and returns candidate-local point clouds plus per-candidate lengths for downstream scoring.

## RRI scoring and label post-processing

- [`OracleRRI`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/rri_metrics/oracle_rri.py#L34) computes the before/after point-to-mesh distances and forms the normalized improvement value.
- The scorer emits [`RriResult`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/rri_metrics/types.py#L39), which keeps the scalar RRI together with the accuracy/completeness diagnostics used elsewhere in the repo.
- Ordinal post-processing is handled by [`RriOrdinalBinner`](https://github.com/JanDuchscherer104/NBV/blob/main/aria_nbv/aria_nbv/rri_metrics/rri_binning.py#L88), which owns fit/transform/save/load behavior for CORAL-ready labels.

# External dependencies we lean on

- **EFM3D** provides `PoseTW`, `CameraTW`, typed batch conventions, and depth/point-cloud helpers that the pipeline reuses rather than reimplementing.
- **PyTorch3D** provides the GPU depth-rendering path and point/mesh distance kernels used by the current scorer.
- **trimesh** is used for GT mesh IO, coarse preprocessing, and geometry utilities before the tensors move into the rendering and scoring stages.

# Where to dive deeper

- [Computing RRI with Ground Truth Meshes](rri_computation.qmd) for the mathematical pipeline description.
- [ASE Data Pipeline & Streamlit Usage](data_pipeline_overview.qmd) for the interactive path through the same surfaces.
- [View Introspection Network (VIN) on EVL](vin_nbv.qmd) for the consumer of the resulting oracle labels.
